# Tasks — Apple Health Multi-Source Health Data Import
*Generated by create_tasks on 2026-04-29*

## Objective
Consolidate Withings, Apple Health, Evernote, and Oura tag data into health.db to enable cross-pillar correlation queries ("do hard workout days predict HRV drops?") via iMessage.

## Scope
- **In Scope**: PRAGMA user_version migration framework; body_metrics, activity_daily, workouts, workout_exercises, oura_tags tables; withings-sync.py cron; import-apple-health.py (all categories); import-evernote-workouts.py; Oura enhanced_tag sync extension; health_query.py subcommands body-metrics/activity/workouts/workout-exercises/tags; AGENTS.md Rule 6b extensions; SKILL.md Intents 9-13
- **Out of Scope**: Pre-2025 Evernote backfill; cross-pillar correlation skill; weekly email updates; iOS Shortcut for ongoing Apple Health sync; DEXA data; Omron sync reliability fix

---

## Architecture Context

### Where This Fits
- Extends `health_db.py` (schema hub at `agents/sample-agent/workspace/health/health_db.py`) with 5 new tables and a migration framework
- New sync scripts in `scripts/` follow the exact pattern of `scripts/oura-sync.py` (token load → paginate → upsert → sync_state)
- New import scripts in `scripts/` follow the exact pattern of `scripts/import-blood-pressure.py` (parse → dry-run → upsert → summary)
- New health_query.py subcommands extend `agents/sample-agent/workspace/health/health_query.py` via existing argparse dispatcher
- Enforcement wired via `agents/sample-agent/workspace/AGENTS.md` (Rule 6b append) and `agents/sample-agent/workspace/skills/health-query/SKILL.md` (new Intent blocks)

### Technical Approach
- **Schema migration**: PRAGMA user_version gating in `initialize_schema()` — version 1 (existing), 2 (body_metrics), 3 (activity_daily + workouts), 4 (workout_exercises + oura_tags)
- **Withings OAuth**: Non-standard flow (`action=requesttoken` in POST body, tokens in body not Basic Auth); raw `requests` preferred over PyPI library; one-time `withings-auth.py` writes tokens to `.env`
- **Apple Health**: stdlib `iterparse` with `elem.clear()` — mandatory for 200MB–2GB files; BP dedup preserves existing omron_csv/imessage rows; body comp preserves withings_api rows
- **Evernote**: Two-pass parse (lxml outer ENEX → html.parser inner ENML tables); filter by title regex `"Week \d+ Training Plan"`; link exercises to workouts by date match
- **Storage units**: All body measurements in lbs (convert kg×2.20462 on import); source column distinguishes withings_api > apple_health priority

### Key Decisions
- Withings API is authoritative for body composition; Apple Health XML is historical backfill — source priority enforced via WHERE guard on DO UPDATE upsert
- Fixed drop folder `~/Downloads/apple_health_export/export.xml` with `--file` override CLI arg
- Evernote access is manual ENEX export (API no longer available); re-export periodically
- Store weight/composition in lbs throughout — consistent with US units used for BP
- AGENTS.md Rule 6b blocks must stay under 4 lines each to manage prompt size (~48K chars, ~17% already truncated)

## Tasks

### Phase 1: Foundation & Withings Body Composition (Session A)

#### [1.1] DB Migration Framework
- [ ] **1.1.1** Add `SCHEMA_VERSION` constant and version-gated migration blocks to `initialize_schema()` in `health_db.py`
  - **Produces**: versioned migration framework used by all subsequent schema additions
  - **Consumed by**: all calls to `health_db.get_connection()` (runs initialize_schema on connect)
  - **Replaces**: unversioned `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` blocks (those remain as safety net but are now gated)
  - [ ] `PRAGMA user_version` reads 0 on existing DB → stamps version 1 without structural changes; all existing tables preserved
  - [ ] Fresh DB runs all version blocks sequentially and lands at current max version
  - [ ] `initialize_schema()` remains callable multiple times (idempotent)

#### [1.2] body_metrics Schema
- [ ] **1.2.1** Add `body_metrics` table in a version-2 migration block within `initialize_schema()`
  - **Produces**: `body_metrics` table with columns: id, date, time, weight_lbs, fat_ratio_pct, fat_mass_lbs, lean_mass_lbs, muscle_mass_lbs, source, fetched_at
  - **Consumed by**: withings-sync.py (writes), import-apple-health.py (writes), health_query.py body-metrics (reads)
  - **Replaces**: N/A (new table)
  - [ ] `UNIQUE(date, time)` index exists; index on `date` column exists
  - [ ] `source TEXT DEFAULT 'withings_api'` column present; `fetched_at` defaults to `datetime('now')`
  - [ ] Existing DB at version 1 migrates to version 2 without data loss; `PRAGMA user_version` reads 2 after connect

#### [1.3] Withings OAuth Setup Script
- [ ] **1.3.1** Create `scripts/withings-auth.py` — one-time browser OAuth flow that writes tokens to `.env`
  - **Produces**: `WITHINGS_CLIENT_ID`, `WITHINGS_CLIENT_SECRET`, `WITHINGS_ACCESS_TOKEN`, `WITHINGS_REFRESH_TOKEN`, `WITHINGS_TOKEN_EXPIRY` entries in `agents/sample-agent/.env`
  - **Consumed by**: withings-sync.py (reads these vars on every run)
  - **Replaces**: N/A (new setup script)
  - [ ] Script opens Withings authorization URL in browser, starts stdlib `http.server` on port 8080 to catch callback, exchanges authorization code for tokens via POST to `wbsapi.withings.net/v2/oauth2` with `action=requesttoken`
  - [ ] Writes/updates token lines in `.env` without clobbering other vars; prints confirmation of expiry timestamp
  - [ ] Uses only stdlib (`http.server`, `urllib.parse`, `urllib.request`, `webbrowser`); no pip deps

- [ ] **1.3.2** Add Withings credential vars to `.env` template and document OAuth developer setup steps
  - **Produces**: documented one-time setup process (in script docstring or a `WITHINGS_SETUP.md` note alongside the script)
  - **Consumed by**: operator (Jeff) running withings-auth.py for the first time
  - [ ] `.env` example/template shows required var names with placeholder values
  - [ ] Setup doc covers: create developer.withings.com app, set redirect URI to `http://localhost:8080/callback`, copy client_id + secret to `.env`, run `python3 scripts/withings-auth.py`

#### [1.4] withings-sync.py — Daily Body Composition Sync
- [ ] **1.4.1** Implement token loading and proactive refresh in `scripts/withings-sync.py`
  - **Produces**: valid Bearer token (refreshed if expiry < now + 5 min) for all API calls
  - **Consumed by**: `fetch_measures()` function within the same script
  - [ ] `load_credentials()` reads 5 vars from env/`.env` using same pattern as `oura-sync.py::load_token()`
  - [ ] `refresh_if_needed()` POSTs `action=requesttoken` + `grant_type=refresh_token` to `wbsapi.withings.net/v2/oauth2` with client_id/secret in body (not Basic Auth header); updates `.env` in-place with new access_token + expiry
  - [ ] Script exits with clear error if credentials missing or refresh fails

- [ ] **1.4.2** Implement `fetch_measures()` with pagination in `scripts/withings-sync.py`
  - **Produces**: list of decoded measure dicts (value decoded as `value * 10^unit`, converted kg→lbs)
  - **Consumed by**: `sync_body_metrics()` function
  - [ ] POSTs to `wbsapi.withings.net/measure` with `action=getmeas` and meastype list `1,5,6,8,76`; paginates via `more` flag + `offset` param until `more == 0`
  - [ ] Decodes each measure: real_value = `value * (10 ** unit)`; converts kg→lbs (`× 2.20462`) for types 1, 5, 8, 76; fat_ratio (type 6) stored as percentage directly
  - [ ] Groups measures by `grpid` (weigh-in session) so one row per session is written to body_metrics

- [ ] **1.4.3** Implement `sync_body_metrics()` with upsert and sync_state tracking
  - **Produces**: body_metrics rows in health.db; updated sync_state entry for resource `"withings_body"`
  - **Consumed by**: main() dispatch loop; health_query.py body-metrics reads the resulting rows
  - [ ] Uses `date_chunks(start, end, days=90)` from oura-sync.py pattern; iterates chunks calling `fetch_measures()`
  - [ ] Upserts to body_metrics with `ON CONFLICT(date, time) DO UPDATE SET ... WHERE body_metrics.weight_lbs IS NOT excluded.weight_lbs OR ...` — skips truly unchanged rows (rowcount=0)
  - [ ] Calls `set_last_synced(conn, "withings_body", today)` after successful sync; prints summary (inserted, skipped)

- [ ] **1.4.4** Add `main()` with `--historical` / `--since` / incremental modes and launchd setup
  - **Produces**: runnable sync script + launchd plist for daily automated execution
  - **Consumed by**: launchd scheduler (host); operator for manual runs
  - [ ] `main()` mirrors oura-sync.py pattern: `--historical` (2020-01-01 start), `--since YYYY-MM-DD`, incremental (reads sync_state)
  - [ ] Launchd plist at `~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.ironclaw.withings-sync.plist` uses `StartCalendarInterval` (daily 6am); script path is absolute
  - [ ] `launchctl load` command documented in script header comment

#### [1.5] body-metrics Query Subcommand
- [ ] **1.5.1** Add `body_metrics()` function and `body-metrics` argparse subcommand to `health_query.py`
  - **Produces**: JSON dict with body composition data, consumed by agent exec in SKILL.md Intent 9
  - **Consumed by**: SKILL.md Intent 9 exec call; AGENTS.md Rule 6b enforcement
  - **Replaces**: N/A (new subcommand)
  - [ ] Args: `--days N` (default 90), `--start YYYY-MM-DD`, `--end YYYY-MM-DD`; `--start`/`--end` override `--days`
  - [ ] Returns `{days_requested, readings, data: [{date, time, weight_lbs, fat_ratio_pct, fat_mass_lbs, lean_mass_lbs, source}], summary: {avg_weight_lbs, min_weight_lbs, max_weight_lbs, avg_fat_ratio_pct, latest: {…}}}` 
  - [ ] Returns `{"error": "no body metrics data in …"}` via `_err()` when no rows; never crashes with unhandled exception

#### [1.6] Session A Enforcement Wiring
- [ ] **1.6.1** Extend Rule 6b in `AGENTS.md` with body composition block
  - **Produces**: enforcement block that forces exec before any body composition response
  - **Consumed by**: GPT-5-mini agent runtime (prepended into system prompt)
  - [ ] New "For body composition" block appended inside existing Rule 6b section; trigger phrases cover: "my weight", "body fat", "lean mass", "fat percentage", "weight trend", "how much do I weigh"
  - [ ] Forbidden phrases list in Rule 6b header already covers this data type (no new forbidden phrases needed if existing list is generic)
  - [ ] Block is ≤4 lines; total AGENTS.md size checked (warn if >50K chars)

#### [1.7] Session A Tests
- [ ] **1.7.1** Write unit tests in `test_withings_sync.py` for decode, conversion, and upsert logic
  - **Produces**: `agents/sample-agent/workspace/health/test_withings_sync.py`
  - **Consumed by**: CI / manual `python3 -m unittest` run
  - [ ] Tests cover: Withings value decode (`value=705, unit=-1 → 70.5`), kg→lbs conversion (tolerance ±0.01), upsert identical row → rowcount 0, upsert changed value → rowcount 1, pagination mock (two API calls → all records collected)
  - [ ] Tests use in-memory DB via `_make_conn()` pattern from test_blood_pressure.py; mock `requests.post` via `unittest.mock.patch`
  - [ ] All tests pass: `python3 -m unittest test_withings_sync`

- [ ] **1.7.2** Write unit tests for body-metrics subcommand in `test_health_query.py`
  - **Produces**: additional test class in existing test file
  - **Consumed by**: CI / manual test run
  - [ ] Tests cover: returns correct JSON structure; `--days` window filters correctly; `--start`/`--end` overrides `--days`; returns error JSON when no data
  - [ ] Monkey-patches `health_query.health_db.get_connection` to in-memory DB per established pattern
  - [ ] Total test suite still passes: `python3 -m unittest test_health_query test_blood_pressure`

---

### Phase 2: Apple Health XML Import (Session B)

#### [2.1] Session B Schema Migration
- [ ] **2.1.1** Add `activity_daily` and `workouts` tables in a version-3 migration block in `health_db.py`
  - **Produces**: two new tables; DB version bumped to 3
  - **Consumed by**: import-apple-health.py (writes), health_query.py activity/workouts (reads)
  - [ ] `activity_daily`: columns id, date (UNIQUE), steps, daylight_minutes, source, fetched_at
  - [ ] `workouts`: columns id, date, start_time, end_time, workout_type, duration_min, calories, avg_hr, max_hr, effort_rating, source, notes, fetched_at; `UNIQUE(date, start_time, workout_type)` index; index on `date`
  - [ ] DB at version 2 migrates to version 3 without data loss; fresh DB reaches version 3

#### [2.2] import-apple-health.py — Streaming Parser Core
- [ ] **2.2.1** Implement `parse_export(filepath)` streaming XML generator using `iterparse` in `scripts/import-apple-health.py`
  - **Produces**: generator yielding normalized dicts per record type + workout; consumed by all category-specific import handlers
  - **Consumed by**: `import_bp()`, `import_body_metrics()`, `import_activity()`, `import_workouts()` functions
  - [ ] Uses `xml.etree.ElementTree.iterparse(filepath, events=("end",))` with `elem.clear()` after each processed element — no full-file memory load
  - [ ] Fixed drop path constant `~/Downloads/apple_health_export/export.xml`; `--file PATH` CLI arg overrides; exits with clear error if file not found
  - [ ] `--dry-run` flag: parse all records, print counts per category (BP pairs, weight, body fat, steps records, daylight records, workouts), exit 0 without DB writes

- [ ] **2.2.2** Implement category routing: dispatch each record type to the appropriate buffer/handler
  - **Produces**: per-category buffers (BP pairs dict keyed by startDate, body comp list, daily steps dict, daily daylight dict, workouts list)
  - **Consumed by**: flush functions that write each buffer to DB after parse completes
  - [ ] BP: buffer systolic and diastolic separately keyed by `startDate` string; flush pairs where both exist
  - [ ] Steps: accumulate per-date sum (multiple sources per day → sum all); flush as daily totals
  - [ ] Daylight: same sum-per-date pattern as steps
  - [ ] Workouts: collect `<Workout>` elements with `<WorkoutStatistics type="HKQuantityTypeIdentifierHeartRate">` child for avg/max HR

#### [2.3] BP Import Handler
- [ ] **2.3.1** Implement `import_bp(conn, pairs)` — upsert BP pairs from Apple Health into `blood_pressure` table
  - **Produces**: rows in `blood_pressure` with `source='apple_health'`; consumed by existing blood_pressure query subcommand
  - **Consumed by**: existing `blood-pressure` health_query.py subcommand (reads all rows regardless of source)
  - [ ] Parses Apple Health timestamp format `"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %z"` → extracts date (YYYY-MM-DD) and time (HH:MM)
  - [ ] Upsert uses `ON CONFLICT(date, time) DO UPDATE SET source=excluded.source WHERE blood_pressure.source IS 'apple_health'` — apple_health rows never overwrite omron_csv or imessage rows at the same datetime
  - [ ] Prints count of inserted vs skipped (already present from other sources)

#### [2.4] Body Composition Import Handler
- [ ] **2.4.1** Implement `import_body_metrics(conn, records)` — upsert weight and body comp from Apple Health into `body_metrics`
  - **Produces**: rows in `body_metrics` with `source='apple_health'`; consumed by body-metrics query subcommand
  - **Consumed by**: `body-metrics` health_query.py subcommand (reads all sources)
  - [ ] Converts `HKQuantityTypeIdentifierBodyMass` (kg) → weight_lbs; `HKQuantityTypeIdentifierBodyFatPercentage` → fat_ratio_pct; timestamps parsed same as BP handler
  - [ ] Upsert WHERE guard: `apple_health` rows do not overwrite existing `withings_api` rows at same datetime (source priority: withings_api > apple_health)
  - [ ] Weight and body fat records may arrive at different timestamps; each is its own row in body_metrics (not joined)

#### [2.5] Activity Import Handler (Steps + Daylight)
- [ ] **2.5.1** Implement `import_activity(conn, steps_by_date, daylight_by_date)` — upsert daily activity totals into `activity_daily`
  - **Produces**: rows in `activity_daily`; consumed by activity query subcommand
  - **Consumed by**: `activity` health_query.py subcommand
  - [ ] Steps: `INSERT INTO activity_daily (date, steps, source) VALUES (?, ?, 'apple_health') ON CONFLICT(date) DO UPDATE SET steps=excluded.steps` — re-import overwrites with fresh aggregate (steps can be corrected by Apple Health retroactively)
  - [ ] Daylight: same upsert pattern for `daylight_minutes` column
  - [ ] Single commit after all dates processed; prints total days imported

#### [2.6] Workout Import Handler
- [ ] **2.6.1** Implement `import_workouts(conn, workouts)` — upsert Apple Watch workout sessions into `workouts` table
  - **Produces**: rows in `workouts` with `source='apple_health'`; consumed by workouts query subcommand
  - **Consumed by**: `workouts` health_query.py subcommand; later linked by import-evernote-workouts.py
  - [ ] Strips `HKWorkoutActivityType` prefix from workout type string (e.g. `FunctionalStrengthTraining`)
  - [ ] Extracts `<WorkoutStatistics type="HKQuantityTypeIdentifierHeartRate">` avg and maximum attributes for avg_hr, max_hr
  - [ ] Upsert on `UNIQUE(date, start_time, workout_type)`; duration in minutes (from `duration` attribute, `durationUnit="min"`)

#### [2.7] activity Query Subcommand
- [ ] **2.7.1** Add `activity()` function and `activity` argparse subcommand to `health_query.py`
  - **Produces**: JSON with daily steps and daylight data; consumed by SKILL.md Intent 10 exec
  - **Consumed by**: SKILL.md Intent 10; AGENTS.md Rule 6b activity block
  - [ ] Args: `--days N` (default 14), `--start`, `--end`, `--metric [steps|daylight|all]`
  - [ ] Returns `{days_requested, days_available, data: [{date, steps, daylight_minutes}], summary: {avg_steps, total_daylight_hours, avg_daylight_min}}`
  - [ ] Returns `{"error": "…"}` when no data; filters by metric if specified

#### [2.8] workouts Query Subcommand
- [ ] **2.8.1** Add `workouts()` function and `workouts` argparse subcommand to `health_query.py`
  - **Produces**: JSON with workout sessions; consumed by SKILL.md Intent 11 exec
  - **Consumed by**: SKILL.md Intent 11; AGENTS.md Rule 6b workouts block
  - [ ] Args: `--days N` (default 30), `--start`, `--end`, `--type TYPE` (filters by workout_type substring, case-insensitive)
  - [ ] Returns `{days_requested, total_workouts, data: [{date, workout_type, duration_min, calories, avg_hr, max_hr, source}], summary: {by_type: {…}, avg_duration_min, total_calories}}`
  - [ ] Returns `{"error": "…"}` when no data; `--type` filter uses `LIKE '%TYPE%'` with wildcard escaping

#### [2.9] Session B Enforcement + Tests
- [ ] **2.9.1** Extend Rule 6b in `AGENTS.md` with activity and workouts blocks
  - **Produces**: two new enforcement blocks in Rule 6b
  - **Consumed by**: GPT-5-mini agent runtime
  - [ ] Activity block: trigger phrases "my steps", "steps this week", "how active", "daylight", "time outside"; exec command `activity --days 14`
  - [ ] Workouts block: trigger phrases "my workouts", "did I exercise", "gym this week", "workout summary", "training"; exec command `workouts --days 30`
  - [ ] Both blocks ≤4 lines; total AGENTS.md size remains manageable

- [ ] **2.9.2** Write unit tests for import-apple-health.py in `scripts/test_apple_health.py`
  - **Produces**: test file verifying all 5 import handlers
  - **Consumed by**: CI / manual test run from scripts/
  - [ ] Tests cover: iterparse on synthetic minimal XML extracts correct records; BP dedup (apple_health doesn't overwrite omron_csv); steps aggregation (3 records same day → sum); workout type stripping; workout HR extraction from WorkoutStatistics child
  - [ ] Integration test: subprocess `--dry-run` against a small synthetic export.xml → exits 0, prints non-zero counts for each category
  - [ ] Tests for body-metrics and activity/workouts subcommands in test_health_query.py added; full suite passes

---

### Phase 3: Evernote + Oura Tags + Full Query Wiring (Session C)

#### [3.1] Session C Schema Migration
- [ ] **3.1.1** Add `workout_exercises` and `oura_tags` tables in a version-4 migration block in `health_db.py`
  - **Produces**: two new tables; DB version bumped to 4
  - **Consumed by**: import-evernote-workouts.py (writes workout_exercises), oura-sync.py tags (writes oura_tags), health_query.py workout-exercises/tags subcommands (reads)
  - [ ] `workout_exercises`: columns id, workout_id (FK → workouts.id ON DELETE CASCADE, nullable), workout_date, exercise_name, set_number, reps, weight_lbs, notes; index on `workout_date`
  - [ ] `oura_tags`: columns id (TEXT PK, Oura-assigned), day, tag_type, start_time, end_time, comment, fetched_at; index on `day`
  - [ ] DB at version 3 migrates to version 4; fresh DB reaches version 4

#### [3.2] Oura Enhanced Tag Sync
- [ ] **3.2.1** Add `sync_tags()` function to `scripts/oura-sync.py`
  - **Produces**: oura_tags rows in health.db; sync_state entry for resource `"tags"`
  - **Consumed by**: main() sync loop (appended after heartrate sync); `tags` health_query.py subcommand reads results
  - [ ] Calls `fetch_all("enhanced_tag", chunk_start, chunk_end, headers)` with `date_chunks(start, end, days=90)`; 404 returns `[]` gracefully (some Oura plans lack this endpoint)
  - [ ] Upserts via `INSERT OR REPLACE INTO oura_tags` using Oura-assigned `id` as PK; maps `tag_type_code` → `tag_type` column
  - [ ] Added to main() sync loop with resource_key `"tags"`; `set_last_synced()` called after success

#### [3.3] import-evernote-workouts.py — ENEX Parser Core
- [ ] **3.3.1** Implement outer ENEX XML parse in `scripts/import-evernote-workouts.py` — extract note metadata and ENML content
  - **Produces**: list of `{title, created_date, content_html}` dicts for notes matching title pattern; consumed by table extraction function
  - **Consumed by**: `extract_workout_tables()` function
  - [ ] Uses `lxml.etree.XMLParser(recover=True, resolve_entities=False)` to handle DOCTYPE without network fetch; falls back to `xml.etree.ElementTree` if lxml unavailable
  - [ ] Filters notes by title regex `r"Week \d+ Training Plan"` (case-insensitive); skips non-matching notes silently
  - [ ] Filters to notes with `<created>` ≥ 2025-01-01 (Evernote format: `20250101T090000Z`)

- [ ] **3.3.2** Implement inner ENML table extraction using `html.parser`
  - **Produces**: list of row dicts `{date, exercises_text}` per note; consumed by exercise parser
  - **Consumed by**: exercise parser and workout linker
  - [ ] Parses CDATA content string with `html.parser.HTMLParser` (stdlib); finds `<table>` elements; extracts rows where col 0 = day and col 2 = "Actual" column text
  - [ ] Skips rows where Actual column is empty, whitespace, or contains only a screenshot reference
  - [ ] Derives absolute date from week number in note title + day-of-week in row col 0

#### [3.4] Exercise Text Parser
- [ ] **3.4.1** Implement `parse_exercise_line(text)` — regex extraction of sets/reps/weight with raw-text fallback
  - **Produces**: list of `{exercise_name, set_number, reps, weight_lbs}` dicts per line; consumed by workout_exercises upsert
  - **Consumed by**: `import_exercises()` function
  - [ ] Primary regex: `(\w[\w\s]+?)\s+(\d+)[×x×](\d+)\s*@?\s*(\d+(?:\.\d+)?)?\s*(lbs|kg)?` captures name, sets, reps, weight, unit
  - [ ] Fallback: if regex fails, creates single row with `exercise_name=raw_text`, `notes=raw_text`, set_number/reps/weight_lbs=None
  - [ ] Weight unit conversion: if `kg` detected, multiply by 2.20462 → weight_lbs

#### [3.5] Note-to-Workout Linking
- [ ] **3.5.1** Implement `link_and_import_exercises(conn, note_rows)` — match Evernote rows to workouts table and write workout_exercises
  - **Produces**: workout_exercises rows linked to workouts.id where match found; orphan rows with `workout_id=NULL` where no Apple Watch workout exists
  - **Consumed by**: `workout-exercises` health_query.py subcommand (reads via workout_date index)
  - [ ] Match strategy: `SELECT id FROM workouts WHERE date=? AND workout_type LIKE '%Strength%'` (or relevant type); if single match, set workout_id FK; if multiple or none, set workout_id=NULL and log warning
  - [ ] Before inserting exercises for a given workout_date, DELETE existing workout_exercises for that date (re-import is idempotent — Evernote is the source of truth for exercise detail)
  - [ ] If no workouts row exists for a Evernote workout date, create a minimal workouts row with `source='evernote'` before linking

#### [3.6] workout-exercises Query Subcommand
- [ ] **3.6.1** Add `workout_exercises_query()` function and `workout-exercises` subcommand to `health_query.py`
  - **Produces**: JSON with exercise detail for a date or window; consumed by SKILL.md Intent 12 exec
  - **Consumed by**: SKILL.md Intent 12; AGENTS.md Rule 6b workout-detail block
  - [ ] Args: `--date YYYY-MM-DD` (single day) or `--days N` (window, default 7)
  - [ ] Returns `{date_range, workouts: [{date, workout_type, exercises: [{exercise_name, set_number, reps, weight_lbs, notes}]}]}`
  - [ ] Returns `{"error": "…"}` when no exercises found; joins workout_exercises with workouts via workout_id or workout_date

#### [3.7] tags Query Subcommand
- [ ] **3.7.1** Add `tags_query()` function and `tags` subcommand to `health_query.py`
  - **Produces**: JSON with Oura tag history; consumed by SKILL.md Intent 13 exec
  - **Consumed by**: SKILL.md Intent 13; AGENTS.md Rule 6b Oura tags block
  - [ ] Args: `--days N` (default 30), `--start`, `--end`, `--type TAG_TYPE` (optional filter)
  - [ ] Returns `{days_requested, total_tags, data: [{day, tag_type, comment}], by_type: {sauna: N, alcohol: N, …}}`
  - [ ] Returns `{"error": "…"}` when no tags found in window

#### [3.8] Full AGENTS.md Rule 6b Wiring
- [ ] **3.8.1** Append workout-detail and tags enforcement blocks to Rule 6b in `AGENTS.md`
  - **Produces**: complete Rule 6b covering all 5 new data types (body-metrics added in 1.6, activity+workouts in 2.9, plus these two)
  - **Consumed by**: GPT-5-mini agent runtime
  - [ ] Workout detail block: trigger phrases "what did I do at the gym", "my exercises", "sets and reps", "strength training detail"; exec `workout-exercises --days 7`
  - [ ] Tags block: trigger phrases "my sauna days", "when did I sauna", "alcohol tags", "Oura tags", "tag trends"; exec `tags --days 30`
  - [ ] All Rule 6b blocks (body comp, activity, workouts, workout detail, tags) are present and under 4 lines each; total AGENTS.md char count verified and acceptable

#### [3.9] SKILL.md Intents 9-13
- [ ] **3.9.1** Add Intents 9-13 to the Intent Classification table and body sections in `SKILL.md`
  - **Produces**: complete skill routing for all new data types
  - **Consumed by**: agent skill trigger matching at message receipt
  - [ ] Intent Classification table has 5 new rows (9-13) following exact format of existing rows
  - [ ] Each intent has: mandatory-stop cap in ALL CAPS, STEP 1 exec in fenced block with default args, STEP 2 synthesize instructions, Hard rules block with explicit error fallback phrase
  - [ ] Intent 9 (body-metrics), 10 (activity), 11 (workouts), 12 (workout-exercises), 13 (tags) all reference correct exec paths and subcommand args

#### [3.10] Session C Tests + End-to-End Deploy
- [ ] **3.10.1** Write unit tests for Evernote parser and exercise text parser
  - **Produces**: `scripts/test_evernote.py`
  - **Consumed by**: CI / manual test run
  - [ ] Tests: note title regex matches "Week 1826 Training Plan", rejects "Shopping List"; exercise parser handles "Squat 3×5 @ 185" correctly; exercise parser fallback stores raw text; two-pass parse on synthetic ENEX extracts correct table rows; date-from-week-number derivation correct
  - [ ] Integration test: subprocess `--dry-run` on real ENEX export prints expected note count

- [ ] **3.10.2** Run full test suite and iMessage end-to-end validation
  - **Produces**: all tests passing; agent responding correctly to all new query types via iMessage
  - **Consumed by**: deployed agent (via `docker restart sample-agent_secure`)
  - [ ] Full test suite passes: `python3 -m unittest test_health_query test_blood_pressure test_withings_sync` (and apple_health/evernote tests)
  - [ ] `docker restart sample-agent_secure` after AGENTS.md and SKILL.md changes; iMessage test sends one query per new data type and confirms exec fires + natural language reply is returned (no "I don't have that on file" responses)
  - [ ] Oura tags appear in health.db after next `oura-sync.py` run (or manual `--historical` run)

---

## Execution Strategies

### Sequential Execution
1. **1.1** DB Migration Framework (no dependencies)
2. **1.2** body_metrics Schema (depends on 1.1)
3. **1.3** Withings OAuth Setup (parallel with 1.2, no DB dependency)
4. **1.4** withings-sync.py (depends on 1.2, 1.3)
5. **1.5** body-metrics subcommand (depends on 1.2)
6. **1.6** Session A Rule 6b (depends on 1.5)
7. **1.7** Session A Tests (depends on 1.4, 1.5)
8. **2.1** Session B Schema (depends on 1.1)
9. **2.2** Apple Health parser core (depends on 2.1)
10. **2.3** BP import handler (depends on 2.2)
11. **2.4** Body comp import handler (depends on 2.2)
12. **2.5** Activity import handler (depends on 2.2)
13. **2.6** Workout import handler (depends on 2.2)
14. **2.7** activity subcommand (depends on 2.1)
15. **2.8** workouts subcommand (depends on 2.1)
16. **2.9** Session B enforcement + tests (depends on 2.3-2.8)
17. **3.1** Session C Schema (depends on 2.1)
18. **3.2** Oura tags sync (depends on 3.1)
19. **3.3** Evernote ENEX parser (depends on 3.1)
20. **3.4** Exercise parser (depends on 3.3)
21. **3.5** Note-to-workout linking (depends on 3.4, 2.6)
22. **3.6** workout-exercises subcommand (depends on 3.1)
23. **3.7** tags subcommand (depends on 3.1)
24. **3.8** Full Rule 6b wiring (depends on 3.6, 3.7)
25. **3.9** SKILL.md Intents 9-13 (depends on 3.6, 3.7)
26. **3.10** Tests + end-to-end deploy (depends on all prior)

### Parallel Execution

**Wave 1** (concurrent, Session A foundation):
- **1.1** DB Migration Framework
- **1.3** Withings OAuth Setup Script
- *Rationale: migration framework has no deps; OAuth setup is standalone browser flow with no code dependencies*

**Wave 2** (after Wave 1):
- **1.2** body_metrics schema (needs 1.1)
- *Rationale: schema must exist before sync or query scripts*

**Wave 3** (concurrent, Session A implementation):
- **1.4** withings-sync.py (needs 1.2, 1.3)
- **1.5** body-metrics subcommand (needs 1.2)
- *Rationale: sync script and query subcommand only share the schema dependency; no direct coupling*

**Wave 4** (concurrent, Session A wiring + tests):
- **1.6** Session A Rule 6b
- **1.7** Session A Tests
- *Rationale: enforcement wiring and tests are independent of each other*

**Wave 5** (Session B schema, can start in parallel with Wave 3):
- **2.1** Session B Schema (needs 1.1 only)
- *Rationale: activity_daily and workouts tables only depend on the migration framework*

**Wave 6** (concurrent, Session B import handlers — all need 2.2 core first):
- **2.2** Apple Health parser core (needs 2.1)
- Then in parallel after 2.2: **2.3**, **2.4**, **2.5**, **2.6**, **2.7**, **2.8**
- *Rationale: BP/body comp/activity/workout handlers are independent category processors; activity and workouts query subcommands need only the schema, not the import handlers*

**Wave 7** (concurrent, Session B completion):
- **2.9** Session B enforcement + tests (needs all of Wave 6)

**Wave 8** (Session C, after Wave 7):
- **3.1** Session C Schema
- Then: **3.2**, **3.3** (concurrent, both need 3.1 only)
- Then: **3.4** → **3.5** (sequential, 3.4 feeds 3.5)
- Then concurrent: **3.6**, **3.7**, **3.8**, **3.9**
- Finally: **3.10** (needs all of Wave 8)

---
